2606.24604.title: Uncertainty-Aware Longitudinal Forecasting of Alzheimer's Disease Progression Using Deep Learning
2606.24604.paper_summary: Longitudinal modelling of Alzheimer's disease progression is clinically useful only if it can describe not just the most likely next diagnosis, but how a patient may evolve over time and how reliable that forecast is. Most deep learning approaches reduce this problem to single-step classification, treating cognitively normal, mild cognitive impairment, and dementia as flat categories while providing limited insight into how uncertainty accumulates across future visits. We propose a probabilistic framework that combines ordinal diagnosis prediction, multi-horizon trajectory generation, and decomposed uncertainty estimation. A Temporal Fusion Transformer encoder is adapted with a CORAL ordinal output layer, asymmetric loss weighting, and converter oversampling to respect disease-stage ordering and improve sensitivity to MCI-to-dementia transitions. Conditioned on the learned patient-context representation, an autoregressive Mixture Density Network generates five-year probabilistic trajectories for diagnosis state, CDR Sum of Boxes, MMSE orientation, and hippocampal volume. On ADNI, the model outperforms linear, recurrent, and transformer baselines for next-visit diagnosis prediction, with the strongest gains on MCI-versus-dementia discrimination. Generated trajectories achieve near-nominal 90% credible interval coverage, widening uncertainty across the forecast horizon, and biomarker dynamics consistent with expected Alzheimer's disease progression. We further separate aleatoric from epistemic uncertainty using analytic mixture variance and a five-member bootstrap ensemble, which provides the strongest encoder diversity and output-level epistemic signal. Epistemic uncertainty is higher for rare progression archetypes, MCI and dementia patients, and under external evaluation on OASIS-3, where it increases alongside prediction error.
2606.24604.paper_summary_zh: 長期模型化阿茲海默症進展的臨床用途僅在於它能夠描述不僅是最可能的下一診斷，還有患者隨時間演變的方式以及該預測的可靠性。大多數深度學習方法將這個問題簡化為單步分類，將認知正常、輕度認知障礙和癡呆視為平面類別，同時對未來訪問中不確定性如何累積提供有限的洞見。我們提出了一個概率框架，結合了序數診斷預測、多時間範圍軌跡生成和分解不確定性估計。一個時間融合Transformer編碼器被調整為具有CORAL序數輸出層、不對稱損失加權和轉換器過採樣，以尊重疾病階段排序並提高對輕度認知障礙到癡呆過渡的敏感性。在學習到的患者上下文表示的條件下，自回歸混合密度網絡生成五年概率軌跡，用於診斷狀態、CDR總和盒、MMSE方向感和海馬體體積。在ADNI上，該模型在下一次訪問診斷預測中超越了線性、遞歸和Transformer基準，對於輕度認知障礙與癡呆的區分獲得了最強的增益。生成的軌跡實現了接近名義的90%可信區間覆蓋，隨著預測範圍的擴大而加寬不確定性，並且生物標記動態與預期的阿茲海默症進展一致。我們進一步使用解析混合方差和五成員自助集成來區分隨機不確定性和認知不確定性，這提供了最強的編碼器多樣性和輸出層認知信號。對於稀有進展典型、輕度認知障礙和癡呆患者，認知不確定性較高，並且在OASIS-3的外部評估中，隨著預測誤差的增加而增加。
2606.24604.author: Arya Hariharan et.al.
2606.24604.authors: Arya Hariharan, Shreyank N Gowda, Anala M R
